text: We don't have much time before the conference.
Interrupt
Unexpected
React
Situation
Urgent
Moment
Calm
Suddenly
At 9 a.m., we were waiting for the rector.
I was presenting the report when the interpreter left.
While we were having dinner, the lights went out.
What were you doing when the news came?
Maria Clara, can I tell you about my trip to Seoul last year?
Of course. What happened?
I was giving a presentation when the projector stopped working. Out of the blue.
Oh no. That is an unexpected situation. How did you react?
For a moment, I was very nervous. But I stayed calm and I kept talking.
While you were talking, was somebody fixing the projector?
Yes. A technician was working on it, and I was speaking without slides.
I think that was very professional. I agree, calm is more important than perfect.
I was preparing the agenda when the phone rang.
It happened out of the blue, but I stayed calm.
While we were having dinner, the rector made an announcement.
For a moment, I did not know how to react.
I am not a beginner anymore. I am improving.

Maria, before your trip, I want to tell you one thing. In every official dinner, something small goes wrong. The interpreter is late, a phone rings, the schedule changes out of the blue. It is normal. When it happens, do not panic. Take one moment, breathe, and speak with simple words. You do not need perfect English. You need calm English. Last year, while we were visiting a university in Lisbon, the microphone stopped working in the middle of my talk. I was not ready for it. But I stayed calm, I smiled, and I kept going. Nobody remembers the microphone. They remember that I finished. You are ready for this. You are not a beginner anymore. You are improving every single day.

I want to tell you about a moment last month. It was a normal Tuesday. I was preparing the agenda for the delegation, and my assistant was checking the flights. Everything was calm. Then, out of the blue, the phone rang. It was an urgent call: our meeting with the council was moving to the next morning. At that moment, everyone in the office reacted. People were talking, the printer was running, and the schedule was changing in front of us. But I did not panic. While my team was calling the hotel, I was writing the new plan. In one hour, the situation was under control. It was not an easy day, but we handled it together, and I am proud of that.

It was the first official dinner of the trip. Maria Clara was sitting next to the rector, and her interpreter was translating every sentence. Then, out of the blue, the interpreter received an urgent message and left the room. For a moment, Maria Clara was alone with the rector. She did not panic. While the waiters were serving the coffee, she stayed calm and she started to speak in simple English.
